A2 Key for Schools — Practice Test 1
Units 1–4: Life in the City · Amazing Jobs · Secrets of the Dark · Living Together
 
 
 
PAPER 1: READING AND WRITING
Time: 1 hour 10 minutes
READING
Part 1   Questions 1–6
For each question, choose the correct answer.
1
City Park Notice
Please keep to the paths and do not walk on the green spaces. Dogs must be on a lead at all times.
You can let your dog run free in the park.
You should stay on the paths and control your dog.
Dogs are not allowed in the park.
2
From: Maya   To: Leo
Hi Leo, I can't come to the community meeting about the new skyscraper tonight. Can you tell me what they decide?
Maya wants Leo to go to a meeting instead of her.
Maya is inviting Leo to a meeting about a building.
Maya wants to know what happens at the meeting.
3
Night Zoo Adventure
Open every Friday evening 7 p.m. – 10 p.m. See nocturnal animals when they are most active! Children under 12 must be with an adult.
The zoo is open every night of the week.
You can visit the zoo on Friday evenings to watch animals that live in the dark.
Children of all ages can visit the zoo alone on Fridays.
4
Swimming Pool
The outdoor pool is closed until March. Indoor pool times are the same as usual.
You cannot swim at all during the winter.
Both pools are closed until March.
You can still use the indoor pool.
5
From: Kenji   To: Sara
I'm doing a school project on urban wildlife. Have you seen any interesting animals in your neighbourhood?
Kenji is asking Sara to help with his homework about animals in cities.
Kenji wants Sara to come and see some wild animals.
Kenji is telling Sara about the animals he found.
6
DANGER — Deep Water
Do not swim here. Go to the beach on the other side of the bridge.
This is a good place for swimming.
You can swim if you cross the bridge.
Swimming is not allowed anywhere near here.
BAYETAV Schools · A2 Key for Schools Practice Test 1 · Paper 1: Reading & Writing · Page 1
Part 2   Questions 7–13
Read the text about three people and their unusual jobs. For each question, choose the correct answer.
A — Tom: Wildlife Photographer
Tom takes photos of wild animals for nature magazines. He travels to forests and oceans around the world. He sometimes waits for hours in the dark to get the perfect photo of a nocturnal animal. Tom says, "My job is dangerous sometimes, but I love it. Last month, I was in the jungle for three weeks to photograph jaguars."
B — Priya: Marine Biologist
Priya studies creatures that live deep in the ocean. She dives underwater to observe animals that create their own light, called bioluminescence. She works at a research centre and teaches students about ocean life. "I became interested in the sea when I was a child," she says. "Protecting ocean animals is the most important part of my job."
C — Daniel: City Park Designer
Daniel designs parks and green spaces in big cities. He works with architects and communities to build places where people and nature can share space. He says, "Cities need more trees and gardens. I want to help people connect with nature, even in the middle of a busy city."
Write the correct letter (A, B or C) in the box.
7
Who works to make cities greener?
8
Who spends time in dark places for work?
9
Who wants to keep animals safe?
10
Who has a job that can be risky?
11
Who learned to love their subject when they were young?
12
Who works together with other professionals?
13
Who studies animals that produce light?
Part 3   Questions 14–18
Read the text and answer the questions. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
London's First National Park City
In July 2019, London became the world's first National Park City. This doesn't mean London is a traditional national park like those in the countryside. Instead, it is a new idea that encourages people to make cities greener, healthier and wilder.

The idea came from a geography teacher called Daniel Raven-Ellison. He walked across London and noticed that more than 47% of the city was already green — parks, gardens, rivers and even trees growing along streets. He thought, "Why not celebrate this and try to make it even better?"

The National Park City plan asks everyone to help. Schools plant trees, communities create small gardens, and people build homes for birds and insects on their balconies. There are also events like night walks in parks to see urban wildlife — foxes, bats and owls that come out after dark.

Not everyone agreed with the idea at first. Some people thought it was strange to call a city a "national park." But Daniel says it is working. Since 2019, thousands of new trees have been planted, and more people are spending time outdoors.
14 What is different about London's National Park City?
A   It is in the countryside.
B   It is a city that wants to become greener and wilder.
C   It has more green space than any other city.
15 Who started the National Park City idea?
A   A group of scientists.
B   The government of London.
C   A geography teacher.
16 What did Daniel Raven-Ellison discover about London?
A   Less than half of the city was green space.
B   Almost half of London was already green.
C   London had no parks or gardens.
17 What can people do to help the National Park City plan?
A   Move out of the city.
B   Plant trees and make gardens.
C   Stop using parks at night.
18 How did some people feel about the idea at first?
A   Everyone loved it immediately.
B   Some people found it unusual.
C   Nobody was interested.
BAYETAV Schools · A2 Key for Schools Practice Test 1 · Paper 1: Reading & Writing · Page 2
Part 4   Questions 19–24
Read the text. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
Animals That Help People

Throughout history, humans and animals have lived (19) ___. Today, some animals are trained to (20) ___ people in special ways. Guide dogs help people who cannot see, and therapy animals visit hospitals to make patients feel (21) ___.

In some cities, there are programmes where people (22) ___ look after rescue animals. These animals were in danger, but now they live safely with families. Scientists say that spending time with animals (23) ___ good for our health. It can reduce stress and make us feel (24) ___.
19.   A apart   B together   C alone
20.   A help   B teach   C follow
21.   A worse   B better   C harder
22.   A must   B should   C can
23.   A are   B is   C was
24.   A happier   B sadder   C angrier
Part 5   Questions 25–30
Read the email. For each question, write ONE word to fill the gap.
From: Emma   To: Jack

Hi Jack,
I went to the new city zoo (25)   Saturday. It was amazing! There (26)   so many interesting animals. My favourite (27)   the nocturnal house, where you can see animals that are active (28)   night. The guide told us a (29)   of interesting facts about bats and owls.

Do you want to come with (30)   next weekend?

Emma
WRITING
Part 6   Question 31
Your English friend wants to know about a place in your city. Write an email to your friend.
Write 25 words or more. In your email:
• say which place in your city you like    • explain why you like it    • invite your friend to visit it with you

Look at the three pictures. Write a story about a school trip to a wildlife park.
